data.title: Testing Traits
data.name: Testing Traits
data.summary:
This is a browser-based cognitive assessment made of ten short games, each mapped to five traits: memory, attention, problem solving, emotional regulation, and cognitive flexibility. The experience is intentionally lightweight, people can start fast, play in any order, and get partial results without being forced into a full run.

It seems aimed at everyday users who are curious about how they perform right now, including students, creators, and professionals who want a structured self-check rather than a clinical diagnosis. The useful part is the mix of simple tasks, visible progress, and trait-level scoring that stays readable and practical, while keeping data local and private.

What is clear is the product’s tone: focused, calm, game-like but not childish, and structured around quick completion. What is still uncertain is scientific validation depth, so I’d describe it as a reflective performance profile tool, not a medical or diagnostic instrument.
data.oneLiner: A game-based cognitive check-in that lets people test memory, attention, flexibility, emotional steadiness, and problem solving in one short, approachable session.

This is a [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) project bootstrapped with [`create-next-app`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/cli/create-next-app).

## Getting Started

First, run the development server:

```bash
npm run dev
# or
yarn dev
# or
pnpm dev
# or
bun dev
```

Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000) with your browser to see the result.

You can start editing the page by modifying `app/page.tsx`. The page auto-updates as you edit the file.

This project uses [`next/font`](https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/optimizing/fonts) to automatically optimize and load [Geist](https://vercel.com/font), a new font family for Vercel.

## Learn More

- [Next.js Documentation](https://nextjs.org/docs) - learn about Next.js features and API.
- [Learn Next.js](https://nextjs.org/learn) - an interactive Next.js tutorial.

You can check out [the Next.js GitHub repository](https://github.com/vercel/next.js) - your feedback and contributions are welcome!
